description_long: This bungalow features a prominent red shingled cross-gabled roof with multiple dormers and a distinctive blue wooden exterior. The front facade is highlighted by a covered porch with white railings and a partially constructed pergola, adding architectural interest. The windows are mostly rectangular and arranged in a somewhat symmetrical pattern, with three dormer windows on the main roof and additional windows on the sides. The structure sits on a raised foundation, and the entryway is accessed by a short set of steps leading to the porch. The roofline is complex, with intersecting gables and a small circular cutout on one side. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a classic American Craftsman bungalow, blending decorative woodwork with practical design.
description: A blue wooden bungalow with a red cross-gabled roof, dormer windows, and a covered front porch with a pergola. The structure features decorative trim and a raised foundation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, covered porch, pergola, and three dormer windows on the roof.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the gabled end, a single large window, and the side of the porch and pergola.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back wall with fewer windows, the side of the roof with dormers, and a small circular roof cutout.
description_view_3: The opposite gabled end is visible, with a single window and the side of the porch and pergola structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing dormer windows with additional side and rear windows
